questions.text: What is the central theme of Surah Ash-Shura regarding revelation and guidance?
questions.answer: That Allah guides through revelation, calling to tawhid, unity in religion, and righteous conduct.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention the heavens almost splitting from awe of Allah?
questions.answer: To show Allah's عظَمة (greatness) and to humble human pride before the Creator.

questions.text: What do angels do for the people of earth according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They exalt Allah and seek forgiveness, highlighting Allah's mercy and the honor of repentance.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah resembles His creation in any way.
questions.answer: False. It states there is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

questions.text: How does Surah Ash-Shura balance Allah's transcendence and His nearness to creation?
questions.answer: It says nothing is like Him, yet He hears and sees all and controls the affairs of His servants.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about unity among believers in the religion of Allah?
questions.answer: That the core religion is one and believers must establish it and avoid division.

questions.text: Why do people often divide after knowledge comes to them?
questions.answer: Because of ظلم and rivalry—wanting dominance rather than submitting to truth.

questions.text: What is one major principle of inter-faith history mentioned by the Surah?
questions.answer: Allah sent the same core message to prophets: worship Allah, establish religion, and avoid sectarian division.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Quran being in Arabic in terms of responsibility?
questions.answer: Clarity removes excuses; people are warned in a language they can understand.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the Day of Assembly as described in this Surah?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment when humanity will be gathered and their outcomes will be decided.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that Allah's mercy is narrow and only for a few.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes Allah's واسع الرحمة (vast mercy) and acceptance of repentance.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about repentance and Allah's response to it?
questions.answer: Allah accepts repentance and pardons many misdeeds when people turn back sincerely.

questions.text: How should believers view calamities according to the Surah?
questions.answer: As partly a consequence of deeds and also as a mercy because Allah pardons much and gives chances to reform.

questions.text: Does the Surah teach that every hardship is pure punishment with no mercy in it?
questions.answer: No. It indicates Allah pardons much and hardship can serve as correction and reminder.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect personal actions to social consequences?
questions.answer: It teaches that people's choices can bring wider consequences, and that reform begins with personal accountability.

questions.text: What is one sign mentioned in the sea that invites reflection?
questions.answer: Great ships moving like mountains, showing Allah's control over nature and human benefit.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah's ability to stop the wind and halt ships?
questions.answer: That everything depends on Allah; He can change conditions instantly, so people should be humble and grateful.

questions.text: How does Surah Ash-Shura define a mature believing community?
questions.answer: They establish prayer, avoid major sins, forgive, consult each other, and spend from what Allah provides.

questions.text: What is the role of consultation (shura) in a believer's life?
questions.answer: It helps decisions be balanced and just, preventing tyranny and selfishness.

questions.text: True/False: Shura replaces revelation and makes everything purely democratic.
questions.answer: False. Shura is used within Allah's guidance, not against it.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about anger management?
questions.answer: Believers forgive when angry, showing strength of character and reliance on Allah.

questions.text: Is forgiving always required, even when oppression continues?
questions.answer: No. The Surah allows just self-defense; forgiveness is praised when it leads to reconciliation and stops wrongdoing.

questions.text: What is the Surah's principle regarding justice and retaliation?
questions.answer: Retaliation should be equal to the harm, but forgiveness and reconciliation are rewarded by Allah.

questions.text: How does the Surah define who is truly blameworthy in conflicts?
questions.answer: Those who oppress people and commit tyranny unjustly; defending oneself from injustice carries no blame.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about worldly life compared to the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Worldly benefits are temporary, while the Hereafter is lasting and should be the believer's priority.

questions.text: What is meant by 'harvest' of the Hereafter in this Surah?
questions.answer: The reward of faith and righteous deeds that Allah multiplies beyond expectation.

questions.text: What happens to someone who only seeks the harvest of this world?
questions.answer: They may receive some worldly benefit, but they lose their share in the Hereafter.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah encourages Muslims to abandon worldly responsibilities completely.
questions.answer: False. It teaches balance: do good, spend, consult, and seek the Hereafter without neglecting duties.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah granting children?
questions.answer: Allah gives daughters, sons, both, or none according to His wisdom; it is part of His decree.

questions.text: How does this teaching protect believers from arrogance or despair regarding family matters?
questions.answer: It reminds them that outcomes are in Allah's hands, so they should be grateful and patient.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the ways Allah communicates revelation?
questions.answer: Through inspiration, from behind a veil, or by sending an angelic messenger.

questions.text: Why is this important for understanding prophethood?
questions.answer: It clarifies that revelation is real and comes through defined means, not imagination or invention.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Quran being a 'light'?
questions.answer: It guides hearts and societies out of confusion and misguidance into clarity and faith.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach humility about guidance?
questions.answer: It shows guidance is a gift from Allah; no one guides themselves purely by their own power.

questions.text: What does the Surah imply about arguing in religion for ego or dominance?
questions.answer: It is condemned; it produces division and ظلم and wastes the purpose of revelation.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah's judgment can be escaped by alliances or status.
questions.answer: False. All matters return to Allah, and no protector can replace Him.

questions.text: How can a community apply shura in modern settings while staying within Islamic principles?
questions.answer: By consulting sincere, knowledgeable people, seeking justice, and keeping decisions aligned with Allah's guidance.

questions.text: What is one personal habit encouraged by this Surah to prevent division?
questions.answer: Purify intentions, avoid envy, and prioritize truth over winning arguments.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah being the final return of all matters?
questions.answer: That all decisions, outcomes, and judgments end with Allah, so people should live with accountability.

questions.text: How does the Surah encourage emotional strength without being oppressive?
questions.answer: It allows just defense but praises forgiveness, balancing strength with mercy.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah's kindness in pardoning 'much'?
questions.answer: That Allah does not seize people for every mistake; He gives room to repent and grow.

questions.text: Why is it important that believers avoid major sins according to this Surah's profile of faith?
questions.answer: Because major sins ruin the heart and community, while avoiding them preserves spiritual health.

questions.text: What is one daily-life application of the Surah's teaching on spending?
questions.answer: Spend responsibly for Allah's sake, support family and community, and avoid stinginess.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the link between prayer and community decision-making?
questions.answer: Prayer builds God-consciousness, and shura channels that consciousness into just decisions.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that forgiveness means denying that injustice happened.
questions.answer: False. Forgiveness can acknowledge wrongdoing while choosing reconciliation when wise.

questions.text: How does the Surah shape a believer's mindset toward setbacks and hardship?
questions.answer: By encouraging self-accountability, repentance, patience, and hope in Allah's pardon.

questions.text: What is one misconception about guidance that the Surah corrects?
questions.answer: That guidance is purely inherited; the Surah shows it is from Allah and must be chosen sincerely.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about arrogance in debates about religion?
questions.answer: It leads to division and injustice; believers should seek truth and unity, not ego.

questions.text: What is the Surah's strongest call to action for the reader today?
questions.answer: Worship Allah alone, build a just character, keep unity, consult wisely, and prepare for the Day of Assembly.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Ash-Shura'?
questions.answer: Consultation.

questions.text: What does the Surah say almost happens to the heavens out of awe of Allah?
questions.answer: They almost part from above as the angels exalt Allah and ask forgiveness for those on earth.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention the Quran being revealed in Arabic?
questions.answer: To warn the 'Mother of Cities' (Makkah) and those around it of the Day of Assembly.

questions.text: Does Allah have any likeness in His creation?
questions.answer: No, 'There is nothing like unto Him' (Laysa kamithlihi shay).

questions.text: What was the command given to all Prophets regarding the religion (Deen)?
questions.answer: To establish the religion and not be divided therein.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who desire the harvest of the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Allah increases for them their harvest.

questions.text: What happens to the harvest of those who only desire the world?
questions.answer: Allah gives them some of it, but they will have no share in the Hereafter.

questions.text: Does Allah accept repentance from His servants?
questions.answer: Yes, He accepts repentance and pardons misdeeds.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach is a reason calamities strike people?
questions.answer: Because of what their own hands have earned, yet He pardons much.

questions.text: What sign of Allah in the sea does the Surah mention?
questions.answer: The ships that run like mountains.

questions.text: What qualities does the Surah mention for those who rely on Allah and live faithfully?
questions.answer: They avoid major sins, forgive when angry, establish prayer, conduct affairs by consultation, and spend from their provision.

questions.text: How should believers handle an injury or oppression?
questions.answer: The retribution for an injury is one like it, but whoever pardons and makes reconciliation—his reward is with Allah.

questions.text: Is there any blame on those who defend themselves after being wronged?
questions.answer: No, the blame is only upon those who oppress people and tyrannize the earth unjustly.

questions.text: To whom does the dominion of the heavens and earth belong?
questions.answer: To Allah alone; He creates what He wills.

questions.text: How does Allah grant children to whom He wills?
questions.answer: He gives females to whom He wills, males to whom He wills, or both, or leaves whom He wills barren.

questions.text: In what three ways does the Surah describe Allah communicating revelation to humans?
questions.answer: By inspiration (Wahi), from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger (angel) to reveal what He wills.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about the Prophet's state before revelation?
questions.answer: He did not know what the Book was nor what faith (Iman) was, but Allah made the Quran a light.

questions.text: Where do all matters eventually return?
questions.answer: To Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Straight Path' mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The path of Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and earth.

questions.text: Why is consultation (Shura) emphasized for the believers?
questions.answer: It is a characteristic of their community and a means of making balanced, collective decisions.
